name: Asbury Federal Credit Union
description_short: Asbury Federal Credit Union is a Washington, DC-based credit union established in 1945, serving members of religious groups with savings accounts, auto loans, and personal banking services.
description_long:
Asbury Federal Credit Union was established in 1945 and is headquartered in Washington, DC at 926 11th Street NW. As a credit union, it operates as a not-for-profit financial institution designed to serve its members rather than external shareholders. The organization currently maintains one physical branch location and serves over 87 loyal members with approximately $336.7 million in assets under stewardship.

The credit union offers a range of personal financial products and services including regular savings accounts, share draft accounts, money market accounts, share certificates (CDs), auto loans (both new and used vehicle), personal loans, unsecured credit cards, and other unsecured lines of credit. Members also have access to online banking, a mobile app available on iOS and Android, online bill pay, financial counseling, financial education resources, and various financial calculators. The credit union emphasizes personalized financial solutions tailored to community needs.

Asbury FCU distinguishes itself through its specialized mission serving members of religious groups, aligning financial services with spiritual values and community connection. As a not-for-profit institution, it advertises competitive rates and lower fees compared to for-profit banks. The credit union provides financial counseling and educational resources to support member financial literacy. Members can access customer service by phone at (202) 783-3308, and the institution maintains a routing number of 254074154.

However, prospective members should note several limitations: the credit union is currently very small with only 87 members and a single branch location, offering minimal physical accessibility. The website indicates the lobby is currently closed and the institution may be experiencing service issues. With zero ATMs available and no dedicated employees listed, operational capacity appears severely constrained. Additionally, interest rates for major loan products are listed as 0.00%, which may indicate data reporting issues or lack of current rate transparency on the public platform.
pros: Established in 1945 with long institutional history and stability
Offers not-for-profit structure with advertised competitive rates and lower fees than for-profit banks
Provides both online banking and mobile app access (iOS and Android)
Includes financial counseling and financial education services for members
Serves specialized community of religious group members with values-aligned services
Online bill pay functionality available to members
Multiple account types including savings, money market, CDs, and share draft accounts
cons: Extremely limited membership base of only 87 members with single branch location restricts growth and service capacity
Zero ATMs available means members cannot access cash outside the main office
Lobby currently closed and service issues reported, limiting in-person banking access
No employees listed (0 as of 2026) raises concerns about operational viability and service delivery
Membership eligibility restricted to members of religious groups, limiting access for general public
best_for: Members of religious communities seeking values-aligned financial services
Existing members of Asbury Federal Credit Union's established membership base
Individuals in Washington, DC area with proximity to the single downtown branch location
Consumers seeking not-for-profit financial institutions with lower fees and community focus
diagnosis: Asbury Federal Credit Union is best suited for existing members of religious communities already part of its limited membership base who prioritize values-aligned banking and lower fees. The primary caveat is that this is an extremely small credit union with severe operational constraints—closed lobby, zero ATMs, single branch, and apparent staffing issues—making it unreliable for general consumers seeking accessible banking services.
